title: Sachiel and the Crash of the Continuity Now Shamshel Too
display_title: Sachiel and the Crash of the Continuity Now Shamshel Too
wikitext:
[]: Sachiel and the Crash of the Continuity (Now Shamshel, Too!)             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Nov 21, 2008 6:58 pm During Unit 01's first fight, Sachiel's design (along with almost everything surrounding it) is probably the most inconsistent thing in the series, not counting EoE. Observe (note that these probably aren't all in chronological order): Here, we see that Sachiel's "ribs" have a ± shape. Unit 01 has a bunch of coloration errors, but we won't get into those. A couple of seconds later, Sachiel's ribcage suddenly has a ҂ shape... on one side, at least. Compare the two images side-by side and you'll also notice that the buildings, the mountains, and the clouds have all moved around a bit. I don't see why they bothered to repaint the scene, as it's the same angle as the previous shot... Why waste the money? Here we have something rather similar to the previous shot, except for the fact that there are now two additional small bones on each side of the, erm... neck... and the ribcage area now connects to the bones on the back. I don't think I even need to talk about this one. Here's one rather similar to the second image, with the exception of the fact that the horizontal ribs are now longer and the spikes jutting out of Sachiel's lower back have switched places. You'd think they'd fix these inconsistencies for Rebuild, right? THEY MADE THEM WORSE. Here's the Rebuild equivalent of the first picture. The neck bones are present where they should be; that's nice. And the ribcage is now more ╪-shaped. Oh boy, they fixed it! BUT WAIT! Two or three seconds later, we have this shot. The geography change is MUCH worse than in NGE (probably because the backgrounds are more detailed)... Most of the buildings in the background are gone, the clouds (which aren't moving while you look at them) are completely different, and the mountains have eroded heavily. Sachiel's "neck" bones are completely gone, and the ribcage is connected at the bottom to the back spikes. The core is also lower and larger. The "neck" bones are back, and the ribcage is still connected to the back spikes... but in a completely different place. At least there aren't any coloration errors this time, but the ribs have changed once again. The ribcage is now a + shape, with only one horizontal rib. Same problems as NGE here, plus a new problem: the spikes now "droop" to the ground instead of aiming the direction they should be. Here we have Sachiel with a disconnected, ∓-shaped ribcage. The horizontal ribs also appear to "cover" the vertical spike, whereas in the previous image the vertical spike clearly covered the horizontals. Just a couple of keyframes and not a single cutaway later and, though the ribcage hasn't changed that much, the "beak" is much longer. Most egregiously, without a cutaway, you can watch Sachiel's two right ribs "morph" into, um... I can't describe it, but here you go: Why? Why did they bother to "fix" certain shots when doing so just leads to even more inconsistencies? I hypothesize that this is the real reason Unit 01 ripped off Sachiel's ribcage: she was sick of the continuity errors it was causing. Oh yeah, and Reichu told me I had to post these: http://evageeks.org/images/production/newtype100pc_140,141_sachiel.gif http://evageeks.org/images/production/sachiel,shamshel_designs_yoshitoh-asari.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 21, 2008 7:11 pm You forgot to do episode 01. WHAT'S WRONG WITH YOU. YOU CAN ACTUALLY SEE WHAT'S HAPPENING IN THE EQUIVALENT CUTS FROM REBUILD. I TOLD YOU THIS!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Nov 21, 2008 7:14 pm You tell me a lot of things. I don't have to listen.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Nov 21, 2008 7:14 pm I don't have much to say except that I don't care much about those details, because when it's animated I really don't pay attention to such things. But your shots proved something that we knew already. That damn dvd image quality is way too dark. That's awful...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Nov 21, 2008 7:22 pm Whoa, as much as I love Sachiel, I've never noticed that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 21, 2008 7:25 pm NAveryW wrote: You tell me a lot of things. I don't have to listen.

[]:             Quote Post by Deathmask » Fri Nov 21, 2008 7:39 pm The action frames in anime will rarely ever be consistent. Almost all anime has at least one fight with sub-par animation or detailing. I never noticed this though, it's kinda interesting now that you point it out.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Nov 21, 2008 7:41 pm Deathmask wrote: I never noticed this though, it's kinda interesting now that you point it out.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 21, 2008 7:53 pm We could potentially occupy ourselves for the next six months or more pointing out animation problems / continuity errors in Evangelion . OH DEAR YUI-SAMA THERE ARE SO MANY. NAveryW wrote: at least NGE was never as bad as that frame where Naruto's head was missing.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Fri Nov 21, 2008 8:04 pm interesting, going off the two linked images I almost wonder if the errors between them had something to do with the flesh of Sachiel not conforming to the bones of sachiel (i.e. sachiel is a skeletal frame with playdoh for the flesh that doesn't necessarily have to stick with the bones). Meh, but that's not important, what's important is how does this affect anyone's understanding of Evangelion? :P

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Nov 21, 2008 8:28 pm Zaque wrote: (i.e. sachiel is a skeletal frame with playdoh for the flesh that doesn't necessarily have to stick with the bones).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 21, 2008 8:40 pm Their actual morphology changes, so that doesn't work -- sorry. what's important is how does this affect anyone's understanding of Evangelion? :P

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Fri Nov 21, 2008 8:54 pm Did Eva-01 always have glow-in-the-dark parts?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 21, 2008 8:57 pm :facefaults into the nearest concrete wall: (Protip: The thread includes images from both the original series and the new movie.)

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Fri Nov 21, 2008 9:12 pm So......no? I remember reading a thread discussing the end of EoE, and people were 100% sure that Asuka's eyes where blue......while the screen shot they were referring to showed her with brown eyes. So I don't know anymore

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 21, 2008 9:31 pm No. But seriously, folks. The Asuka thing is an example of people gruesomely overanalyzing a lighting situation. They need to be sent to a color theory class or something.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Fri Nov 21, 2008 9:32 pm CONGRATULATIONS, YOU'VE SHOWN THAT EVANGELION COULDN'T POSSIBLY HAVE BEEN FILMED AND ROTOSCOPED IN THE REAL WORLD! CARE TO PROVE THE MOON LANDINGS WERE FAKE?

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Fri Nov 21, 2008 9:37 pm CorporalChaos wrote: CARE TO PROVE THE MOON LANDINGS WERE FAKE?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Nov 21, 2008 9:44 pm CorporalChaos wrote: CARE TO PROVE THE MOON LANDINGS WERE FAKE?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Nov 21, 2008 9:44 pm I don't think I even need to talk about this one.
